entry.title: Re: Soon to be retried: C_HRHFC - Implementation Consultant - SAP SuccessFactors Full Cloud/Core Hyb
entry.content: Recently 3 of my certifications expired. FI, CO, Treasury and risk managment.( March 31st). Last year i had taken an assessment and extended by an year. This year i missed the dates as i just got my learning hub access. Is there a possibility of getting this extended by taking an assessment ? Please advise.

entry.title: Physical Floor Reality vs SAP EWM Bin Coordinates — Go-Live Risks Nobody Talks About
entry.content:
After 13 years managing warehouse label deployments across hundreds of distribution facilities for Fortune 500 companies, here’s what actually causes physical layer failures at go-live — and it’s not what most SAP consultants expect.
The real issues I’ve seen on the floor:
Structural conflicts — A pillar, beam, or obstruction exists exactly where SAP has a bin location defined. The bin is in the system but physically unreachable or unlabelable. This has to be caught in a site audit before labels are printed.
Coordinate orientation mismatches — If SAP EWM has bin 02-02-D-01 mapped to the east side of the building but the physical layout starts numbering from the west, every single location in that section is wrong. The system is perfect. The floor is perfect. They just don’t match each other.
Label quality failures — Wrinkles during application, ink registration issues from the printer, oil or dust on rack surfaces causing adhesion failure. A label can look installed and still fall off or fail to scan within days.
AI system considerations — As warehouses move toward AI-driven WMS, label placement precision becomes more critical. The system may expect a label in a specific position for automated scanning. A worker can scan a label whether it’s left, center, or right. An automated scanner may not.
The physical layer has to be audited before a single label is printed — not after.
Has anyone developed a pre-go-live physical audit process that accounts for structural conflicts and coordinate orientation before cutover?

entry.title: Certification
entry.content:
HI Community,

I have given my Certification exam for C_TS462_2601, foe which cut off is 82%.

I have scored 81.63%. still system has considered it as failed.

It should be rounded off to 82%. What are your views ?

entry.title: Supplier invoice clear issue-items have not been activated due to inconsistent withholding tax info
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

When we generated the invoice, the WHT details had not been entered in the 'Supplier Master.' However, we have now updated the WHT details in the Supplier Master. After entering the WHT information, we attempted to clear this invoice using a 'down payment' via the 'Clearing Outgoing Payments' application. At this stage, we encountered an issue; we received an error message stating, "Items were not activated due to inconsistent withholding tax information."

Based on my analysis, it appears that the absence of WHT details at the time the invoice was generated is the root cause of this problem.

How can we resolve this issue? Please provide appropriate advice.

Thanks & Regards,

DNR

entry.title: Re: CL_ABAP_PARALLEL: Parallel Processing Without the aRFC Enable Function Modules
entry.content: Thank you Jelena for sharing them.

entry.title: Re: How to use ERS with Service PO
entry.content:
Hi, Services do have GR. 

When a Service Entry sheet is accepted, a GRN is generated in the background. 

The reference field in the SES is used to provide vendor reference which gets populated in Delivery note field in GR. 

ERS  can be used for services too directly in the standard.

Best regards, Ramki

entry.title: Back to the Future
entry.content:
Dear SAP Business One community

Today I want to talk about how you could leverage the recurring transaction functionality within the web client.

Let’s look at this video to learn more:

 

You can find additional information in the SAP Business One Web client user guide  and the Learning journey.

Best wishes,  Ari

entry.title: Re: Scaling SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Let's Collect UI/UX Improvement Ideas Together
entry.content: When I talk to new users, the most intimidating aspect of Cloud ALM is "who does what." It is a tool that touches so many different roles, and people aren't sure which parts of Cloud ALM is their responsibility.

entry.title: Re: Expose Joule Studio Agent/Skills as API for UI-trigge...
entry.content: This functionality is currently not available...  The Agent Gateway is not yet GA.  Perhaps the SAP Product team can comment on this and provide more guidance.

entry.title: Re: automate the marriage leave
entry.content:
Yes, this requirement can be achieved using Integration Center. 

You can create an Integration Centre job where the source object is Personal Information, and the target object is Employee Time. Based on the mapping, the system can automatically create a Marriage Leave record.

Set the Start Date as the Effective Date (Marital Status Since) from Personal Information. The End Date can be derived based on company policy (for example, Start Date + 5 days).

In the filter section
Maintain Marital Status = Married to pick only relevant employees
Use the time-based filter Modified Since Last Run Time to ensure only newly updated records are processed and to avoid duplicate leave creation

Once the job is scheduled, whenever an employee updates their marital status to “Married”, the system will automatically create the corresponding Marriage Leave record in Employee Time.

entry.title: Re: Validation before putaway while creating task for Aut...
entry.content:
Good day @Harry94 

Thank you for your question.

The object you mentioned is a classic EWM enhancement object which is under the /SWCM/. However, identifying an alternative enhancement point or BAdI for custom bin filtering would be an EWM ABAP enhancement topic. This type of enhancement is available in an on-prem/private cloud environment where you have the possibility to carry out ABAP enhancement implementations.

So, in this case, the Public Cloud does not offer you a standard Fiori configuration or key user extensibility. In a standard cloud setup, you should check custom bin filtering against standard EWM configuration options, e.g., storage section search, bin type determination, putaway control indicator, etc.

So, if your requirement is to inject custom ABAP logic into automatic bin determination, then consider on-prem or private cloud.

 

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Building an AI Agent for SAP Maintenance using MCP, CAP and Claude Desktop
entry.content: It is possible on ECC as well?

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps - upload files to SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX) Service with camera

  • List tests done with postman and store 
  • send request as shown with msg created

but, there is a but it seems that the pict send by my phone is not in the good format. If i check in the SAP Document AI UI, I have FAILED and this message :"The file format isn't supported, or the file content doesn't match the file extension." I do not know how to solve this

entry.title: Re: How to connect SAP Gateway OData source with Angular
entry.content:
Hello,

Can I use SAML2 to authenticate the user ?

entry.title: Re: Requirement for uploader against this app - Enter Ass...
entry.content:
Good day @K12 

Thank you for your question.

I am not aware of any option in the Public Cloud that offers standard configuration to add a custom uploader directly to Enter Asset Transaction: Transfer Within Company Code. The standard rout is for you to validate whether the requirement can be covered by Mass Processing of Assets or by a released fixed asset API.

You can use Fixed Asset Master Data API (API_FIXEDASSET_G4BA) under Asset Accounting APIs and communication scenario SAP_COM_0A93. You should check this for supported master data operations, but do not assume that you can replace every filed and posting behaviour of the transfer transaction app.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/74ffc024fd794eeca49dc8ffe7f9870d/46a9aa881ff7417c86bae1ee9a700673.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/5bcca49998834de2b5533c36602e52a2/560ade3edf964466b10cc918c50cfa8e.html?locale=en-US

Here is my next step recommendation:

  1. Test one manual transfer in Enter Asset Transaction: Transfer Withing Company Code and document the required fields.
  2. Search for Mass Processing of Assets and check whether the mass transfer template covers the same fields.
  3. Check API_FIXEDASSET_G4BA only for supported fixed asset master data operations.
  4. If your required mass transfer posting is not covered by the standard template or released API, consider raising a case with SAP and provide your details. If a gap is confirmed, then raising an influence for a standard mass upload option may be viable.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content: User ID: AC300864U71

entry.title: Provide a CDS view for Overhead rate in Costing Sheet
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,

My project utilizes a costing sheet based on overhead quantity. At the end of each month, a step must be taken to input the actual overhead rate into the system, based on the overhead group maintained. This allows the customer to request a report to verify the actual overhead rate. Please provide a CDS view for the overhead rate in the costing sheet that you have released in the SAP public cloud.

Warm regards,

Chi

entry.title: Re: Characteristic Description Blank
entry.content:
Hello Roberto,

Strange it is! This may not be a direct answer to your issue but wanted to include screen-shots which comments do not allow

Did a little more digging and this may be a stretch in terms of being too technical but was checking how this information is pulled in the background and it seems there is a Function module CLSE_SELECT_CABNT(Routine F01_SELECT_CABNT) that seems to be used to fetch the description. I know you already mentioned that even at the table level(CABN) the you've probably already checked but maybe worth to have a technical person/developer help you check if the values are indeed fetched correct here:

Regards,
Vijay

entry.title: Agentic AI on BTP: a Single Agent with LangGraph
entry.content:
Introduction

In a recent blog post, https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/odata-mcp-proxy-introduction/ba-p/14348684 , I introduced the OData MCP Proxy: a small server you deploy on BTP that turns any OData service into a fully‑featured MCP server using nothing but a configuration file. That proxy became the backbone of five BTP‑specific MCP servers I published on GitHub:

  • ci-mcp-server — SAP Cloud Integration (github.com/lemaiwo/ci-mcp-server)
  • ai-core-mcp-server — SAP AI Core (github.com/lemaiwo/ai-core-mcp-server)
  • btp-mcp-server — BTP Core Services (github.com/lemaiwo/btp-mcp-server)
  • btp-auditlog-mcp-server — BTP Audit Log (github.com/lemaiwo/btp-auditlog-mcp-server)
  • btp-cf-mcp-server — Cloud Foundry V3 (github.com/lemaiwo/btp-cf-mcp-server)

That got me to a powerful but awkward place. Each MCP server lives on its own URL, has its own OAuth flow and exposes its own catalogue of tools. If a user wants to ask a natural‑language question that spans two or three of them, an MCP client has to juggle multiple connections and multiple logins. For a demo that is fine. For something I would hand to an operations colleague or a business user, it really isn’t.

 

Why

My goal was simple: give a single conversational UI that covers my whole BTP landscape. A user should be able to say "show me the failed integration flows of the last hour" or "which AI Core deployments are running in my dev subaccount?" and get an answer without ever thinking about which API that maps to. Because the user is already authenticated to BTP, the agent should reuse that identity and forward the JWT to every downstream MCP server. No extra logins. No ambient service accounts with god‑mode. Just the user’s own permissions, extended by natural language.

The fact that everything runs inside BTP is the key here. When the agent, the MCP servers and the target APIs share the same XSUAA tenant, propagating the token is straightforward and each downstream service naturally enforces the caller’s authorizations.

 

How

btp-agent is a CAP TypeScript monorepo that hosts both the agent and the chat UI. On the model side I use a LangGraph StateGraph: the graph takes the user message, calls a tool‑enabled LLM, routes to tool invocations and loops back until the model is satisfied with its answer. The LLM itself is AzureOpenAiChatClient from @sap-ai-sdk/langchain, which targets AI Core’s Generative AI Hub through the service binding the CAP app already owns. Tools are not hand‑coded: they come in through @langchain/mcp-adapters MultiServerMCPClient, which spawns MCP servers over streamable HTTP connections to the deployed MCP servers in production. The result is that the agent sees the union of every tool exposed by the five MCP servers and LangGraph handles the orchestration.

Figure 1 — btp-agent solution diagram (L2). Editable .drawio version included with this post.

Figure 2 — Screenshot of the running chat UI (placeholder — replace with your own screenshot before publishing).

 

Key components

srv/agent.ts — the graph

This is where the StateGraph is assembled. The model node calls the chat model with the current message history and the bound tools; the tool node executes any tool calls and appends their results to the state; an edge sends control back to the model until it returns a plain message. Thanks to LangGraph this is around forty lines of code.

srv/mcp/ — the client side

MultiServerMCPClient is initialised with a configuration that points at each MCP server. Locally, it can run with an approuter and the mcp proxy server packages so I can develop without deploying anything. In production the same configuration flips to destinations with the deployed URLs, and the chat service injects the caller’s JWT as a bearer token so the downstream MCP server authorises the user that actually asked the question.

srv/chat-service.ts + chat-service.cds — the shell

A CAP service wraps the agent behind a CDS action and streams tokens over WebSocket, so the UI feels responsive. xs-security.json defines three role templates (MCPViewer, MCPEditor and MCPAdmin) and mta.yaml binds xsuaa-application, destination-lite and connectivity-lite so identity and private‑network access work out of the box when the app is deployed to Cloud Foundry.

 

What I learned (and why this isn’t the final form)

Technically this works. A single agent with tools from five MCP servers can answer real questions about a real BTP landscape. But the pattern has two problems I hit quickly. First, LangGraph has to send the full tool list to the model on every turn and five MCP servers together push well past a hundred tools. The model starts is limited to the amount of tools, latency grows and the prompt footprint becomes uncomfortable. Second, there is no separation of concerns: a single agent prompt is responsible for deciding whether the question is about integration flows, AI deployments or CF apps. As soon as you add a sixth MCP server it gets worse.

That combination pointed clearly to the next step: split one agent into multiple specialists, one per MCP server and put an orchestrator on top. That is what the next post in this series is about.

 

Solution & references

Build & deploy

This repository is a CAP TypeScript monorepo, so an install at the root pulls dependencies for every module (the CAP service, the five MCP servers, the approuter, and the UI5 chat app). Prerequisites: Node.js 20+, the Cloud MTA Build Tool (mbt), and the Cloud Foundry CLI with the MultiApps plugin.

Local development:

git clone https://github.com/lemaiwo/btp-agent.git
cd btp-agent
npm install
cds watch          # serves the CAP app + chat UI on http://localhost:4004

Build and deploy to BTP Cloud Foundry:

npm run build              # tsc && cds build
npm run build:btp          # mbt build  -> mta_archives/btp-agent_1.0.0.mtar
cf login -a <api-endpoint> -o <org> -s <space>
npm run deploy:btp         # cf deploy mta_archives/btp-agent_1.0.0.mtar

The MTA ships nine modules (approuter, CAP srv, UI5 chat, HANA deployer, and four MCP-server apps) and binds xsuaa, HANA HDI, destination, connectivity, html5-repo (host+runtime) and AI Core (extended plan). After deploy, assign the MCPViewer / MCPEditor / MCPAdmin role collections from xs-security.json to the users who should use the chat.

Code: github.com/lemaiwo/btp-agent

Previous post: OData MCP Proxy — Introduction

In the next post I’ll show how splitting the agent into specialists with Pydantic AI fixed the tool‑overload problem and introduced a new one around OAuth: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/agentic-ai-on-btp-a-multi-agent-app-with-pydantic-ai/ba-p/14388029#M178663

entry.title: Re: Imputed interest
entry.content: How can i make imputed interest zero in supplier balance report

entry.title: Agentic AI on BTP: a Multi‑Agent App with Pydantic AI
entry.content:
Introduction

In the previous post I built btp-agent: a single LangGraph agent in a CAP app that wires up five BTP MCP servers (Cloud Integration, AI Core, BTP Core, Audit Log, Cloud Foundry V3) and lets users chat with their full BTP landscape. It worked, but it hit some limitations. With all five MCP servers plugged in, the model saw well over a hundred tools on every turn. Latency climbed, the model reached number of tool limits and the single orchestrator prompt got asked to be an expert in integration flows, AI deployments, audit events and CF applications all at once. For a live demo that is survivable. For anything that wants to scale, it isn’t.

So I took the agent apart.

 

Why

The natural cut line is the MCP server: each server already represents a bounded domain (CI, AI Core, BTP Core, etc.), and each one has its own vocabulary of tools. If every MCP server gets its own specialist agent, each agent’s prompt becomes short and focused, each agent’s tool list becomes small and the model can actually reason about which tool to call. An orchestrator agent on top then decides which specialist(s) to delegate to for a given question and stitches the answers together.

I switched the stack from CAP/TypeScript to Python with Pydantic AI for this step. Pydantic AI gives me typed agents, typed tool calls and first‑class MCP client support, which means each specialist is about thirty lines of code. It also made it natural to treat delegation as just another tool on the orchestrator, which is a pattern that maps cleanly onto how LLMs already think about tool use.

 

How

btp-multiagent-app keeps the same philosophy as before, everything runs on BTP, everything reuses the user’s identity but the internals are rearranged. A chat entry point (app.py) receives the user’s message and hands it to the orchestrator. The orchestrator (agents/orchestrator.py) has no tools of its own other than a set of delegation tools pointing at the specialists:

Figure 1 — btp-multiagent-app solution diagram (L2). Editable .drawio version included with this post.

Figure 2 — Screenshot of the chat UI delegating across specialists (placeholder — replace before publishing).

  • Cloud Foundry specialist: operations against CF V3 via its MCP server
  • BTP specialist: BTP Core Services via its MCP server
  • Audit log specialist: scaffolded and disabled while I iterate on scopes

Each specialist wraps an MCP client that speaks Streamable HTTP to its MCP server, authorised with OAuth 2.1. A small shared module (agents/shared.py) centralises the AI Core GenAI Hub model setup, the MCP client factory and the OAuth flow, so every specialist is stamped from the same template. A legacy agent.py sits next to the new agents/ package as a reference to the single‑agent design from the previous post, useful when explaining the migration.

Key configuration patterns

The app supports two credential layouts for AI Core so it runs comfortably both locally and on CF:

  • Individual environment variables — AICORE_AUTH_URL, AICORE_CLIENT_ID, AICORE_CLIENT_SECRET, AICORE_BASE_URL, AICORE_RESOURCE_GROUP.
  • A single AICORE_SERVICE_KEY JSON — the whole service key dropped in as one variable, handy on CF where a service binding gives you exactly that shape.

MCP server URLs are injected the same way: MCP_CLOUDFOUNDRY_URL, MCP_BTP_URL and MCP_AUDITLOG_URL. Locally you get them by running the MCP servers on 127.0.0.1; on CF the mta.yaml descriptor wires them to the deployed hosts. The AI Core binding is attached automatically so you don’t have to ship credentials into the image.

 

What the orchestrator actually does

The orchestrator is deliberately small. Its prompt tells it: you are a BTP operations assistant; you have the following specialists available; when a user asks a question, decide which specialist(s) can answer, call them, and combine their output into a single response. Under the hood, each delegation tool invokes the corresponding specialist agent with a sub‑prompt, collects the result and returns it to the orchestrator. That lets the model reason at two levels (routing at the top, tool selection inside each specialist) which is exactly what the single‑agent design was trying and failing to do in one go.

The outcome is measurable. Tool lists per agent drop from "well over a hundred" to "a dozen or two", token usage per turn drops with them and the agent stops hallucinating tool names because each specialist only knows the tools that actually make sense for its domain. Debugging also gets easier: when something goes wrong, you can tell whether the orchestrator mis‑routed or the specialist mis‑used a tool, just by reading the trace.

 

What I learned (and the OAuth honesty section)

The multi‑agent pattern pays for itself instantly. Tool lists per agent drop from "well over a hundred" to "a dozen or two", token usage per turn drops with them, no tool-overload and the model stops hallucinating tool names because each specialist only sees the tools that make sense for its domain. OAuth 2.1 against the MCP servers also works fine once deployed, Pydantic AI ships a web client that handles the consent flow inside the running app, so the same code path that works locally works on Cloud Foundry.

What this iteration did make obvious is that the list of specialists is still compiled into the code. Add a sixth MCP server and you have to edit the application, rebuild and redeploy. Fine while the list is short, not a pattern that scales to something like an AI Data Enabler exposing dozens of APIs to LLMs, which is where the next post goes.

 

Solution & references

Build & deploy

This one is a Python FastAPI app with a Node.js approuter in front of it. Prerequisites: Python 3.11+, Node.js 20+ (for the approuter), mbt and the Cloud Foundry CLI with the MultiApps plugin.

Local development:

git clone https://github.com/lemaiwo/btp-multiagent-app.git
cd btp-multiagent-app
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
# point the app at your AI Core + MCP servers
export AICORE_SERVICE_KEY='{"clientid":"...","clientsecret":"...","url":"...","serviceurls":{"AI_API_URL":"..."}}'
export MCP_CLOUDFOUNDRY_URL=https://<your-cf-mcp>.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/mcp
export MCP_BTP_URL=https://<your-btp-mcp>.cfapps.<region>.hana.ondemand.com/mcp
python -m uvicorn app:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 7932

On first chat, each MCP client triggers the OAuth 2.1 browser flow against localhost:3000/callback. Tokens are cached afterwards.

Build and deploy to BTP Cloud Foundry:

mbt build                                        # -> mta_archives/*.mtar
cf login -a <api-endpoint> -o <org> -s <space>
cf deploy mta_archives/<name>.mtar

The MTA contains two modules, the Python FastAPI app (pydantic-agent, started as python -m uvicorn app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port $PORT) and the approuter which binds xsuaa (application plan) plus AI Core (extended plan). MCP server URLs are injected as env vars per module. Reminder: production CF cannot use the localhost OAuth2 callback; switch to client-credentials or pre-provisioned tokens before you promote the deployment.

Code: github.com/lemaiwo/btp-multiagent-app

Previous post: Building a BTP Agent with LangGraph

Next up: making the set of specialists dynamic, so new agents can be created through an admin UI without redeploying the app: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-members/agentic-ai-on-btp-dynamic-multi-agent-on-demand-with-pydantic-ai/ba-p/14388032#M178664

entry.title: Agentic AI on BTP: Dynamic Multi‑Agent on Demand with Pydantic AI
entry.content:
Introduction

Post one introduced the OData MCP Proxy and gave us five BTP MCP servers by configuration. Post two took those MCP servers and wrapped them in a Pydantic AI multi‑agent application: an orchestrator on top, one specialist per MCP server below. That fixed the tool‑overload problem the original single‑agent design had hit. But it left one uncomfortable property in place: the list of specialists was still hard‑coded. Adding another MCP server meant editing the repo and redeploying. For my own BTP management experiments that is acceptable. For a broader scenario (say, an AI Data Enabler that wants to expose many, many APIs to LLMs safely) it very clearly isn’t.

So in this final iteration I made the agents themselves first‑class, dynamic objects: created, edited, deleted and reloaded without ever restarting the app.

 

Why

Two forces push you towards this design. The first is scale. If every domain, every data product, every API surface deserves its own specialist agent, you don’t want to ship a new release every time someone publishes a new API. You want the platform to accept an agent definition the same way a data platform accepts a new table. The second is governance. Treating agent configurations as data (rather than code) means you can export them, version them, review them, diff them and re‑import them. It also gives you a natural place to attach role‑based access: who can create agents, who can only consume them, who can re‑load the orchestrator.

btp-dynamic-multiagent-app takes those two ideas seriously. Every agent lives as a row in a database. A small admin UI lets an authorised user create, edit and disable agents and a Reload button rebuilds the orchestrator in memory without restarting the container.

 

How

The request path is straightforward. A user hits the approuter, the approuter terminates XSUAA authentication and forwards the request to a FastAPI app. Inside FastAPI there are two logical surfaces: /chat, served by the dynamic orchestrator, and /admin, served by a CRUD API plus a small HTML admin page. Both sit behind the same JWT; the admin routes additionally require an admin scope so the UI can only be used by users with the right role collection.

Figure 1 — btp-dynamic-multiagent-app solution diagram (L2). Editable .drawio version included with this post.

Figure 2 — Screenshot of the admin UI adding a new agent (placeholder — replace before publishing).

The pieces that make it dynamic:

  • agents/db.py: SQLAlchemy models for the agent configuration (name, description, instructions, MCP URL, enabled flag). PostgreSQL in production, SQLite locally so you can iterate without infra.
  • agents/registry.py: a builder that reads the current set of enabled agent records, constructs Pydantic AI agents from them on the fly, and produces a fresh orchestrator with delegation tools pointing at each specialist.
  • agents/admin.py: a REST API for CRUD plus a Reload endpoint that calls into the registry to swap in the newly built orchestrator.
  • agents/auth.py: XSUAA JWT validation and a contextvar‑bound token forwarder so every MCP call made during a request carries the original user’s identity.
  • agents/chat_app.py: a dynamic ASGI wrapper for the chat interface so the underlying agents can be swapped at runtime.
  • templates/admin.html + agents.seed.json: the admin UI and a bootstrap configuration for cold starts.

 

How an operator adds a new agent

In the admin UI an operator clicks "+ New agent", fills in a name, a short description, the system instructions for that specialist and the URL of its MCP server. Saving writes a row. Clicking "Reload agents" asks the registry to rebuild the orchestrator; the new specialist appears in the delegation tool set from the very next chat message. No redeploy. No CF restart (although there is an optional Restart‑app button that can trigger one, if you have a technical‑user service binding to the CF API and you actually want that).

On top of the live CRUD you get export and import of the full agent set as a JSON snapshot. That is small but it is what turns agent definitions from "config in a live DB somewhere" into "versionable artefact you can put in Git". It is also how you seed a new environment from a known‑good baseline rather than clicking your way through twenty entries.

 

Security and identity propagation

Because everything runs on BTP behind the approuter, identity is not something this app invents. Every request already arrives with a validated XSUAA JWT; agents/auth.py pins it into a contextvar for the duration of the request. When a delegated specialist calls its MCP server, the middleware grabs that token and forwards it as a bearer credential. The MCP server authorises using the caller’s actual permissions, not a shared service account. The admin surface is gated by a dedicated "Pydantic Agent Administrator" role collection, so only authorised users can create or modify agents. Normal chat users never see the admin UI.

 

Why this matters for the AI Data Enabler

This is the design I want for any serious "expose‑your‑APIs‑to‑LLMs" story. A centralised MCP server that tries to host tools for every API in the organisation becomes a hot mess very quickly: the tool list explodes, governance becomes all‑or‑nothing, and a single team ends up owning everything. A dynamic multi‑agent app inverts that. Each API surface can publish its own MCP server, owned by its own team, registered as an agent in the platform by its owner and consumed through the same orchestrator. The orchestrator sees a small, stable set of specialists; specialists see small, focused tool lists; identity flows through on every call. You can scale the number of exposed APIs without scaling the complexity of any single component.

 

Lessons learned

  • Treating agent definitions as data is a much bigger win than it sounds like. Export/import, diffing, code review, all of it becomes free once the definition lives in a row instead of in a module.
  • Reloading an agent graph in place is subtle. The trick was to keep the chat ASGI wrapper thin and always resolve the current orchestrator lazily, so swapping the instance is atomic from the request’s point of view.
  • Role‑based admin access from day one avoids retrofits later. If the platform can edit agents, it needs a scope for it, full stop.
  • Using SQLite locally and PostgreSQL on CF kept the inner loop fast without forcing a separate "dev" code path.

 

Solution & references

Build & deploy

Same FastAPI + approuter shape as btp-multiagent-app, with a PostgreSQL service added for the agent registry. Prerequisites: Python 3.11+, Node.js 20+, mbt, and the Cloud Foundry CLI with the MultiApps plugin.

Local development:

git clone https://github.com/lemaiwo/btp-dynamic-multiagent-app.git
cd btp-dynamic-multiagent-app
python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
# local uses SQLite automatically — no Postgres needed to iterate
export AICORE_SERVICE_KEY='{"clientid":"...","clientsecret":"...","url":"...","serviceurls":{"AI_API_URL":"..."}}'
python -m uvicorn app:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 7932
# then open http://127.0.0.1:7932/admin to seed agents from agents.seed.json

Build and deploy to BTP Cloud Foundry:

mbt build                                        # -> mta_archives/*.mtar
cf login -a <api-endpoint> -o <org> -s <space>
cf deploy mta_archives/<name>.mtar

The MTA contains the same pydantic-agent Python module and pydantic-agent-approuter Node.js module, and adds a PostgreSQL (postgresql-db, standard plan) resource for the agent registry alongside xsuaa and AI Core. After the first deploy: assign the "Pydantic Agent Administrator" role collection to the users who should be able to create or modify agents, then open /admin to import your agents.seed.json baseline.

Code: github.com/lemaiwo/btp-dynamic-multiagent-app

Editable diagram: 03-btp-dynamic-multiagent-architecture.drawio (shipped alongside this post).

Previous posts in the series: OData MCP Proxy Introduction, btp-agent, btp-multiagent-app.

With this iteration the loop closes: any OData service can become an MCP server with a config file; any MCP server can become a specialist agent with a row in a database and the orchestrator keeps the user experience to a single conversation.

I am trying to develope an API provider in APIM, to connect to iflow, but I can not change it type to clound integration iflow (it produces cloud integratoin only).

entry.title: Exporting SAP Build Apps: Converting "Web and Mobile" to "Web Only" for Build-Default Plan
entry.content:
Hello,

I am moving my development work between tenants. My original project was created as a "Web and Mobile Application" in a tenant with a Free Plan. My new tenant is on the Build-Default plan.

When attempting to import my project, I am blocked by the issue described in KBA 3693320, as the Build-Default plan does not support "Web and Mobile" project types.

Since there is no "Convert to Web-Only" button in the UI, I am looking for the most efficient way to take my work with me.

I would like to know:

  1. Is there any way to export the project so that the target tenant accepts it as a "Web-Only" app?

  2. If direct import is impossible, is there a supported (or community-tested) way to copy objects, logic, or pages from the "Web and Mobile" project and paste them into a fresh "Web Only" project shell?

Thank you for your help!

entry.title: How do you handle tickets when no one on the team knows the solution?
entry.content:
Hi all,

Something I've come across in a few projects and curious if others face this too.

When a ticket is raised and the person who originally built or configured
that part of the system is unavailable — on leave, moved to another project,
or no longer with the company — how does your team handle it?

Especially in cases where there's no documentation, no handover, and
essentially no one who knows why something was configured a certain way
or what the right fix is.

What's the usual approach? How long do these typically take to resolve
compared to a normal ticket?

Genuine question — would appreciate honest experiences from anyone
who has dealt with this.

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge April 2026 - build AI Agents with Generative AI Hub
entry.content: week 2 and 3

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule Document Grounding: What I Learned the Hard Way
entry.content: Good info

entry.title: Re: How can I get Account dimension hierarchy in Datasphe...
entry.content:
Hi Sreekanth, sure. This is in DSP possible with Hierarchy. Either use Hierarchy with Directory, if you have different G/L Ledgers, or use basic Hierarchy. You create G/L Account Hierarchy and ASSOC it to the G/L Account. When using in the AM, the Hierarchy will sum-up the according G/L Accounts. I am using such scenario in an account based AM, based on a KPI Hierarchy I created. Working in DSP flawless. 

In case you already have the Hierarchy in SAC, you can load it to DSP via OData connection. Kind of write-back from SAC to DSP. 

So, create a View type of Hierarchy, or Hierarchy with Directory. Assign as ASSOC in Model-View (FACT level) and just consume via AM in a SAC Story. G/L Account to nodes mapping can be done via CSV (for business the easiest way). Rest is DSP logic. 

Cheers, Martin

entry.title: Re: How can I get Account dimension hierarchy in Datasphe...
entry.content: Thanks @Martin_Kuma, GL account hierarchy is just an hierarchy that can be used for rollup. But we will need to have a calculations/Formulas on the nodes of that hierarchy and I can't find a way to achieve that in Datasphere. Basically I am looking for account dimension feature of SAC that can have formulas.

entry.title: Re: How can I get Account dimension hierarchy in Datasphe...
entry.content: Hi, I do have the same need: sac has a dimension type “account” that let to create calcumated members (gross margin on net Sales or % contribution etc). In a seamless model, how to led datasphere View to inherit and consume calculated members?

entry.title: Re: Introduction to Next-Gen SAP Ariba
entry.content: Can you share the information about the subscription model and further usage cost consumptions

entry.title: Free Learning Materials or Courses for SAP HCM for S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I'm a student and I’m currently preparing a university presentation about SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA (not SuccessFactors), and I’m honestly struggling to find beginner-friendly learning materials.

Our assignment is less about the technical implementation side and more like a sales/business presentation about SAP HCM for S/4HANA — what the module does, which HR/business processes it supports, how it integrates with other SAP modules, and what advantages or use cases it offers for companies. So we basically need to “sell” SAP HCM in a presentation, even though we are complete beginners with SAP. (Please don’t ask why — that’s simply the assignment)

Everything I find on SAP Learning is focused on SuccessFactors. The HCM learning page on SAP is basically only SuccessFactors content. But our presentation is supposed to cover SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA.

The problem is:

  • we are complete beginners with SAP
  • the official documentation is too technical for both, learning and a foundation for the presentation
  • the Global Bike case studies we got are a starting point, but nothing more
  • books are very expensive
  • free beginner resources for SAP HCM for S/4HANA seem almost nonexistent
  • the official learning site for S/4HANA covers all modules like Finance, Asset Management, Supply Chain, Sales, but no Human Capital Management
  • the official learning site for HCM covers only SuccessFactors.

From what I understood, SAP HCM for SAP S/4HANA is still supported until 2040, but finding understandable learning material for it is surprisingly difficult.

So I wanted to ask:

  • Are there any good free resources, courses, YouTube channels, blogs, or demo systems for learning SAP HCM basics?
  • Which older SAP ERP HCM materials are still relevant for S/4HANA?
  • What topics would you recommend focusing on for a university presentation aimed at non-SAP people?
  • Any advice for understanding the modules/system architecture without prior SAP knowledge?

At this point even older but still understandable and applicable materials would help a lot.

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Re: OData V4 Fiori Elements: "KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext" when selecting Valu
entry.content: Hi @TuncayKaraca , Thanks for your reply, I tried removing it and as well having the additional binding, the behavior is still the same.

entry.title: OData V4 Fiori Elements: "KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext" when selecting Value Help - Fiori
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am facing a persistent UI5 ERROR in an OData V4 Fiori Elements application (based on ABAP RAP).

The Scenario: I have a Root Consumption View with an input Parameter. I have assigned a custom CDS Value Help (Dropdown / #XS) to this parameter. The dropdown successfully opens and displays the data in the Fiori Preview.

The Error: When the user clicks/selects an item from the dropdown list, the slection doesn't happen with the following console error in the browser:

Uncaught (in promise) Error: KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext

1. Value Help CDS View (Single Key, Dropdown)

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Value Help for Parameter'
@ObjectModel.resultSet.sizeCategory: #XS  

define view entity ZI_VH_CustomValue
  as select from custom_domain_table
{
      @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 10 }]
        key cast( domain_value as abap.char(1) ) as ValueKey,

      @UI.lineItem: [{ position: 20 }]
      @Semantics.text: true
      domain_text as Description
}
where active_status = 'A'

2. The Consumption View (Short Version)

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Main Business Object'
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true

define root view entity ZC_MainBusinessObject
  with parameters

    @EndUserText.label: 'Custom Filter Parameter'
    @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [{
        entity: { name: 'ZI_VH_CustomValue', element: 'ValueKey' },

        /* Explicit binding to pass the value back to the parameter */
        additionalBinding: [{
            localParameter: 'p_custom_filter',
            element: 'ValueKey',
            usage: #RESULT
        }]
    }]
    p_custom_filter : z_custom_data_element

  as select from ZI_MainBusinessObject
{
  key DocumentId,
      DocumentType,
      PostingDate
}
where PostingDate <= $session.system_date

My Questions:

  1. Is there a known limitation or missing @ui/ @Consumption annotation required to bind a Value Help strictly to a PARAMETER (rather than a standard table field) in OData V4?
  2. Why is the OData V4 framework failing to resolve the context for the selected item despite the explicit localParameter binding?

Any insights or workarounds would be highly appreciated.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: OData V4 Fiori Elements: "KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext" when selecting Valu
entry.content: @TuncayKaraca Here you go! https://community.sap.com/t5/welcome-corner-discussions/odata-v4-fiori-elements-quot-keypath-missing-at-p-getitemfromcontext-quot/m-p/14388116#M10596

entry.title: Re: OData V4 Fiori Elements: "KeyPath missing at p.getItemFromContext" when selecting Valu
entry.content: https://community.sap.com/t5/frontend-ui5-sap-fiori-blog-posts/odata-v4-fiori-elements-quot-keypath-missing-at-p-getitemfromcontext-quot/bc-p/14388117#M85

entry.title: How to delete a custom field created via Coding Block in SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud 2025
entry.content:
We created a field via Coding Block transaction OXK3 in development system. It is not extended to the screens yet.

Due to requirement change, we do not need this field any more. The ACDOCA table contains only 2 entries in DEV environment. There is no data saved in the custom field. I DON'T want to move this field to further systems.

Can I delete this field from coding block?

From all the analysis I have done, I understand that this will impact the tables at database level. But, that can be managed by re-generating the database tables and views. 

Since only development system is impacted with little or no data in the tables, I don't think it would have any impact as such on the system. I would like to know some expert thoughts on deleting custom field created via coding block in a fairly new development system.

entry.title: Re: Building an AI Agent for SAP Maintenance using MCP, CAP and Claude Desktop
entry.content:
Thank you for sharing this interesting architecture.

One point worth clarifying is its alignment with SAP API Policy v.4.2026a. The revised policy appears to restrict SAP API usage where semi-autonomous or generative AI systems plan, select, or execute API call sequences, unless this is done through SAP-endorsed architectures or service-specific pathways.

Given that this setup uses Claude Desktop + MCP + CAP to invoke SAP APIs, would this be considered a permitted production architecture by SAP, or should it be treated only as a non-production PoC/demo?

entry.title: Re: Delete a Z coding block field created by OXK3 transaction
entry.content: This option is not available

entry.title: Re: RE: Coding Block field deletion and adding in Fast Entry screen
entry.content: Was this done on S/4 HANA Cloud? What version did you carry out this activity on?

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content:
Done. UserID GE306937

Change 1.

Change 2.

entry.title: Re: BADI to update inbound Purchase Requisition from IBP...
entry.content:
You can find all available BAIDs on ECC side from below note and community.
BAdIs for MRP - SAP Community
2268085 - S4TWL - MRP Live on SAP HANA - MD01N - SAP for Me

  • MD_PURREQ_CHANGE and MD_PLDORD_CHANGE: Can be used for fill and change fields of purchase requisitions and planned orders created, changed or deleted during the MRP run.
  • MD_PURREQ_POST and MD_PLDORD_POST: Can be used for additional actions after the purchase requisition and planned order changes, such as saving information on a custom table, a workflow or implementing a change log. It should not be used for changing the order itself.

entry.title: Re: Building an AI Agent for SAP Maintenance using MCP, CAP and Claude Desktop
entry.content: Hi @vikrant_guptarya it’s technically feasible, but in practice it’s probably not worth the effort. Which tends to add quite a bit of overhead for this kind of scenario. On BTP, this pattern is much more straightforward you can spin up a CAP server in minutes, which makes the whole setup much easier and faster to get running.

entry.title: Support Pack 35 release query for SAP NetWeaver 7.5 Java system
entry.content:
Hi Team ,

Hope you're well

We are currently on SAP NetWeaver 7.5 SP33 (SAP BOBJ Portal – Java system) and are planning to upgrade to SP34. Could you please confirm if there is any planned release timeline for SP35? Additionally, we would like to understand whether it is recommended to proceed with SP34 or wait for the next support pack.

Thanks & Regards ,

Anitha

entry.title: Re: Building an AI Agent for SAP Maintenance using MCP, CAP and Claude Desktop
entry.content:
Hi @Andre_Sousa1  good point, and honestly something I started thinking about a few weeks ago when I first had the idea for this project. I came across the new SAP API Policy just yesterday and did some research around it and yes, there are real implications for this kind of integration in its current form.

That said, this project is purely a demo. Nothing running in production, no real customer data involved.

I believe SAP will move in this direction regardless  the momentum around AI agents is too strong to ignore. It will be interesting to see what official tooling and endorsed architectures they release. Plenty of room for new ideas to emerge from here.

entry.title: Re: PSM Budget Availability Control
entry.content:
Hi Derraschouk,

GL‑based budget control is currently not available in Public Cloud system.

For feature request, I would recommend raising it via the CIP:

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/1169

 

Our product management team will review and reply to you there.

 

Best Regards,
Owen

entry.title: Re: RFM_ALLOCATION_STRATEGY BAdI
entry.content: Hello Chris, Thanks a lot for clarification and the helpful documents. We'll check with SAP. Regards, May

entry.title: Reviving the SAP Community in Charlotte — Let’s Restart the Conversation
entry.content:
Hello Charlotte SAP Community,

I’m excited to step in as the new curator for our local group. Over the past few years, this space has been quiet, but Charlotte and the Carolinas have a strong SAP footprint — from utilities and manufacturing to financial services and public sector organizations.

My goal is to bring this community back to life by creating a place where we can share experiences, ask questions, highlight local expertise, and learn from each other.

To kick things off, I’d love to hear from you:

What SAP topics, challenges, or innovations are most relevant in your day‑to‑day work right now?

Some ideas to get the conversation started:

  • S/4HANA migration experiences

  • SAP BTP use cases

  • Clean core strategies

  • Integration challenges

  • AI, Joule, and automation

  • Industry‑specific SAP scenarios in the Carolinas

Whether you’re an architect, developer, functional consultant, Basis/security expert, or business user — your voice matters here.

Looking forward to reconnecting and rebuilding this community together.

— Atul, Curator, SAP Community Charlotte Group

entry.title: Change company Industry Type
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I observed that the industry information currently maintained in the company details is showing as a wrong industry type.

Kindly let us know the procedure to update the industry information, or please update it from your end to reflect the correct industry as “XXXXXXXXX”

Regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Change company Industry Type
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Kindly know we SAP Support cannot assist with any customer number related information change, like company address change, company name change and company industry information change.

Only the SAP Order Management or your SAP Account Manager/Executive can assist here, and this is documented in KBA 3748719 - How to change the company industry information of your customer number - SAP for Me.

Could you pls kindly refer to the KBA and contact the SAP Order Management (per KBA 1660069) or contact your SAP Account Manager (per KBA 2626344)?   

Thanks and best regards,
SAP Support

But somehow my custom Sap Object Type is still not appear on configuration. Have you managed to find the answer for this?

Thanks,

Yoppie

We need to approve purchase oder through an extranal system,

approval , or is there another way to handle approvals via en extranal system?

I found the nots 3551832 - Purchase Requisition approval via API and that mentions Purchase Requisition not have api for approval .However,it does not mention whether purchase oder are supported.

entry.title: Re: Sales quotes or sales order replication from sales cl...
entry.content: Hi Dequan, are you talking about S/4hana or S/4Hana public cloud? IDOC setting etc seem to be S/4HANA so as a conclusion it's not possible to replicate sales quotes to v2.

entry.title: Batch Management Conversion Service in S/4HANA Public Cloud not available (as of April 2026)
entry.content:
Following a direct inquiry with SAP DMLT (Data Management and Landscape Transformation), we received confirmation that the Batch Management Conversion Service (as referenced in SAP KBA 533276 – Setting the batch management requirement) is currently not supported for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud — this reflects the current status as of April 2026.

SAP DMLT clarified that Public Cloud systems are outside their service scope, and therefore this conversion service cannot be provided at this time.

I am posting this here for transparency and awareness, in case this info is also relevant to other Public Cloud customers.

 

References:

  • 533276 - Setting the batch management requirement - SAP for Me

entry.title: Re: Unable to upload best practice 2VO's test script...
entry.content:
Good morning,

PLease have a look to SAP Community Blog 

New options to import manual Test Cases into SAP Cloud ALM

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: PSM Budget Availability Control
entry.content: Dear Liu, Thank you for your feedback. Indeed, this is a critical business requirement that requires immediate attention. I have just submitted the official Improvement Request as suggested. I look forward to receiving positive updates regarding this feature in the near future. Best regards https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/373026

entry.title: Re: Configuration parameter [execution]/default_statement_concurrency_limit set to unsupported value
entry.content: Which value is set for default_statement_concurrency_limit?

entry.title: Re: CL_ABAP_PARALLEL: Parallel Processing Without the aRFC Enable Function Modules
entry.content:
Yes, why posting duplicate content without added value? For instance, CL_SSI_DISPATCH and RS_ABAP_PARALLEL have already been mentioned by human at SAP, here repeated without any explanation. I don't see the interest of mentioning them. Less clear than other blog posts, like mentioning both P_NUM_TASKS and P_NUM_PROCESSES, the latter being obsolete (still maintained for old applications using only P_NUM_PROCESSES).

Help - SAP Community:

« If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag GenAI Assisted Content. »

entry.title: SAPSR3./BI0/SAC_DOC_NO partition issue
entry.content:
Dear Team,

Our BW * system is currently generating alerts indicating that the table SAPSR3./BI0/SAC_*** (partition 0) contains 1,9*** records, which is causing performance concerns.

We kindly request you to provide the appropriate online partitioning command that can be executed without downtime.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: SAPSR3./BI0/SAC_DOC_NO partition issue
entry.content:
For you issue you are raising in this case, firstly I think you need perform a redistribution for the whole system in case this has not been performed.

- note
1908075 - SAP BW on SAP HANA: Table Placement and Landscape Redistribution
2334091 - SAP BW/4HANA: Table Placement and Landscape Redistribution

The Redistribution will handle all tables in this system, and this is stongly suggested for BW system.

In case you just want to handle the individual table SAPSR3./BI0/SAC_*** at this moment,
you could follow below note to perform table reorg.

- note
2019973 - Handling Very Large Data Volumes in SAP BW on SAP HANA
-> Run a landscape redistribution as described in SAP Note 1908075. 
As of HANA 1.0 SPS 10, you can restrict a landscape redistribution to a table group which corresponds to a BW object. 
To do this, proceed as follows:

entry.title: Re: Week 4: AI Developer Challenge SAP-RPT-1 Model
entry.content: Here is my entry for week 4.

entry.title: Re: Adding tags to Solution Processes using API
entry.content:
Good morning, 

I cannot see that tags can be added, I was checking the content in Custom Processes API:  https://help.sap.com/docs/CloudALM/fe419bfabbdc46dfbddbfd78b21483d5/401ee1b0dc9c48b7b9a0a54874a9939d.html?locale=en-US&version=LATEST 

But please read this idea and vote it in the SAP Influence if it fits your request:

Get / Post Tags via APIs https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/334862/?section=sectionDetails

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Re: Standard Return Order - Issues
entry.content:
Good day @Rajkumar1989 

Thank you for your question.

The first thing i suggest you check for high volume return order creation, is the Import Customer Returns app. The app can create return orders from spreadsheet files, which is the cleanest standard option when you need to create return orders with many line items.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/ecf09844bd054048b7ad6cd6712f55bb.html?locale=en-US

If you want automatic delivery and credit memo behaviour, the standard process should be controlled using return order release, billing block, and billing due list processing. Note that the customer returns process continues from returns order to returns delivery and goods receipt after release. Lean customer returns contain a billing block to prevent direct credit memo creation.

For reference, consult https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/ef17554b70b946e588cf4fb378fa4622.html?locale=en-US

For FOC lean returns, ensure you only use the supported item category and copy control combinations. There is a limitation for return and credit memo processing with free of charge item categories CBXN/CRXN. See KBA 3741451 for more insights on that. The supported standard free of charge item categories and the limitation when copy control for unsupported item categories is not maintained as standard is described in KBA 3454852.

If the copy control or process combination you require is not available, you cannot freely add the new copy control entries and the missing copy control highlights that the process is not supported. In that case, I suggest you submit a feature request.

Further reference below for more details.

3210945 - Delivery type & cannot be invoiced with billing type & - SAP for Me

To summarise:

  1. Test Import Customer Returns with a small 3 to 5 item upload file.
  2. Confirm whether all mandatory item fields are available in the template.
  3. Check return release and billing block behaviour before allowing follow on delivery or credit memo processing.
  4. Review any scheduling billing jobs if credit memos are automatically being created.
  5. Test FOC lean returns only with supported item categories.
  6. Consider raising a Customer Influence request or a case if the upload fields you require is not supported.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Workzone feature request channel in SAP influence opp...
entry.content:
Hi

This looks like some bug/error
I am reviewing all Work Zone feature requests, the system is available for me with the full list of requests. Also, the channel is very active.. 
I suggest to try again later, and if the problem persists for you, open an issue
Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to Configure and Install People Intelligence in SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
@RACHID_AITBOUALE Thanks for your comments. Currently we have no official reference statistics for derived data products with benchmark with the facts you mentioned above.

But my experience is that the performance driven factors are not multi-country or legal entities itself, rather the data volume.  Most of these insights are quite aggregated, trend analysis with monthly snapshots, thus changing configurations in derived data products and waiting for several hours are good enough.

entry.title: Re: F110_Vendor Debit line items not pick up in Payment p...
entry.content:
Perform the below checks

  1. Run FBL1N for the vendor to confirm the debit items are open and due.
  2. Check if a payment block is set on those items.
  3. Review FBZP settings for the payment method and company code.
  4. In the proposal log (within F110), look for messages explaining why items were excluded.

entry.title: Implementing Chained Actions in Unmanaged SAP RAP
entry.content:
Introduction:

A chain action is when one RAP action calls another action internally using EML: 

Why Chained Actions in Unmanaged RAP?

  • No automatic save handling (you control everything)
  • No framework-managed validations
  • Risk of multiple DB updates / inconsistent state
  • Chaining ensures:
    • Single transactional flow
    • Controlled execution order
    • One final Modify Entities

Architecture Overview: 

UI / EML Call
→ Behaviour Definition (Action Triggered)
→ Behaviour Handler (Main Action Method)
→ Execute Child Action 1 (Validation / Check)
→ Execute Child Action 2 (Preparation / Data Collection)
→ Transactional Buffer (All changes stored in memory)
→ Final MODIFY ENTITIES (Single update)
→ Saver Class (Save Sequence)
→ Database Commit

Implementation: 

  • Step 1: Define Actions In BDEF 
    •  Declare :
      • One Main Action
      • One or More Child Actions

  • Step 2: Implement child Actions in BP class 
METHOD checkBeforeDelivery.

  LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).

    READ ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY yi_salesorder
      FIELDS ( Status BillingStatus )
      WITH VALUE #( ( %tky = ls_key-%tky ) )
      RESULT DATA(lt_data).

    READ TABLE lt_data INTO DATA(ls_data) INDEX 1.

    IF ls_data-Status = 'Delivery Created'.

      APPEND VALUE #( %tky = ls_key-%tky ) TO failed-yi_salesorder.

      APPEND VALUE #(
        %tky = ls_key-%tky
        %msg = new_message(
          id       = 'YCHAINED_MESSAGE'
          number   = '001'
          severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
          v1       = 'Delivery already created'
        )
      ) TO reported-yi_salesorder.

    ENDIF.

    IF ls_data-BillingStatus = 'GENERATED'.

      APPEND VALUE #( %tky = ls_key-%tky ) TO failed-yi_salesorder.

      APPEND VALUE #(
        %tky = ls_key-%tky
        %msg = new_message(
          id       = 'YCHAINED_MESSAGE'
          number   = '002'
          severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error
          v1       = 'Bill already generated'
        )
      ) TO reported-yi_salesorder.

    ENDIF.

  ENDLOOP.

ENDMETHOD.
METHOD GenerateBill.

  LOOP AT keys INTO DATA(ls_key).

    APPEND VALUE #(
      %tky = ls_key-%tky
      %param-BillingStatus = 'GENERATED'
    ) TO result.

  ENDLOOP.

ENDMETHOD.
  • Step 3: Implement Main Action 
METHOD createdelivery.

  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    EXECUTE checkBeforeDelivery
    FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    FAILED DATA(lt_v_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_v_reported).

  IF lt_v_failed IS NOT INITIAL.
    failed-yi_salesorder   = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( failed-yi_salesorder ) lt_v_failed-yi_salesorder ).
    reported-yi_salesorder = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( reported-yi_salesorder ) lt_v_reported-yi_salesorder ).
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    EXECUTE GenerateBill
    FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_invoice_result_raw)
    FAILED DATA(lt_chain_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_chain_reported).

  failed-yi_salesorder   = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( failed-yi_salesorder ) lt_chain_failed-yi_salesorder ).
  reported-yi_salesorder = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( reported-yi_salesorder ) lt_chain_reported-yi_salesorder ).

  IF lt_chain_failed IS NOT INITIAL.
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.

  DATA(lt_billing_result) = lt_invoice_result_raw.

  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    UPDATE FIELDS ( Status BillingStatus )
    WITH VALUE #( FOR key IN keys (
      %tky = key-%tky
      Status = 'Delivery Created'
      BillingStatus = VALUE #(
        lt_billing_result[ %tky = key-%tky ]-%param-BillingStatus OPTIONAL )
      %control-Status        = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
      %control-BillingStatus = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
    ) )
    FAILED DATA(lt_u_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_u_reported).

  failed-yi_salesorder   = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( failed-yi_salesorder ) lt_u_failed-yi_salesorder ).
  reported-yi_salesorder = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( reported-yi_salesorder ) lt_u_reported-yi_salesorder ).

  IF lt_u_failed IS INITIAL.

    READ ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY yi_salesorder
      ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_result).

    result = VALUE #( FOR ls_res IN lt_result (
      %tky   = ls_res-%tky
      %param = ls_res
    ) ).

  ENDIF.

ENDMETHOD.
  • Step 3.1: Execute First Action 
  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    EXECUTE checkBeforeDelivery
    FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    FAILED DATA(lt_v_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_v_reported).
  • Step 3.2: Stop on Error
  IF lt_v_failed IS NOT INITIAL.
    failed-yi_salesorder   = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( failed-yi_salesorder ) lt_v_failed-yi_salesorder ).
    reported-yi_salesorder = CORRESPONDING #( BASE ( reported-yi_salesorder ) lt_v_reported-yi_salesorder ).
    RETURN.
  ENDIF.
  • Step 3.3: Execute Second Action
  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    EXECUTE GenerateBill
    FROM CORRESPONDING #( keys )
    RESULT DATA(lt_invoice_result_raw)
    FAILED DATA(lt_chain_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_chain_reported).
  • Step 3.4: Final Update
  MODIFY ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
    ENTITY yi_salesorder
    UPDATE FIELDS ( Status BillingStatus )
    WITH VALUE #( FOR key IN keys (
      %tky = key-%tky
      Status = 'Delivery Created'
      BillingStatus = VALUE #(
        lt_billing_result[ %tky = key-%tky ]-%param-BillingStatus OPTIONAL )
      %control-Status        = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
      %control-BillingStatus = if_abap_behv=>mk-on
    ) )
    FAILED DATA(lt_u_failed)
    REPORTED DATA(lt_u_reported).
  • Step 3.5: Return Result to UI 
    READ ENTITIES OF yi_salesorder IN LOCAL MODE
      ENTITY yi_salesorder
      ALL FIELDS WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
      RESULT DATA(lt_result).

    result = VALUE #( FOR ls_res IN lt_result (
      %tky   = ls_res-%tky
      %param = ls_res
    ) ).
  • Final Flow 

Main Action(create Delivery) 
  ↓ 
EXECUTE action1 (Validation-checkBeforeDelivery) 
  ↓ 
EXECUTE action2 (GenerateBill) 
  ↓ 
Final MODIFY (Update Fields) 
  ↓ 
SAVE → DB 

  • Output:

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  • Conclusion

Chain actions in RAP provide a clean and structured way to execute multiple steps within a single transactional flow. By orchestrating actions through EML, you can break complex logic into smaller, manageable units while still maintaining consistency. 

The architecture ensures that all steps run inside the same transactional buffer, and only the final state persists through a single update and save sequence. This avoids common issues like buffer overwrites and inconsistent data.
